conflicts.description: The prior sheet reduced Viktor's death to dying at the grate; the manuscript shows he is still hauling on the loading-dock panel when a Lena enforcer kills him with a fire extinguisher blow to the back of the head.
conflicts.canonical_value: Viktor dies on the loading dock when a Lena enforcer crushes the back of his head with a fire extinguisher during the grate assault.
conflicts.resolution_note: The manuscript stages the grate work and the fatal blow in one causally central sequence; the earlier sheet preserved the setup but lost the killing mechanism.

conflicts.description: The prior sheet said Chen Wei died verifying a loading-dock relay path. Chapter 11 makes the attempt more specific: Noor proves the relay rack itself is receive-only, and Chen Wei then tries to verify a possible outbound ingress / junction-box path near the dock.
conflicts.canonical_value: Chen Wei dies at the loading dock while trying to verify a possible outbound path at the ingress / junction box, not at the relay rack itself.
conflicts.resolution_note: Chapter 11 explicitly separates Noor's receive-only rack finding from Chen Wei's remaining ingress hypothesis, so the sheet now records the narrower, better-supported attempt.

conflicts.description: The prior sheet introduced the fire extinguisher in chapter 7, but the manuscript already has one used against Diego's sealed pod in chapter 3.
conflicts.resolution_note: Chapter 3 already places a wall extinguisher on page in active use, so chapter 7 was too late to remain canon.

conflicts.description: The prior sheet introduced the galvanized steel pipe in chapter 17, but Priya retrieves it from the closet as hardware-assault weapon stock left there three days earlier.
conflicts.resolution_note: Chapter 17 explicitly back-references the pipe to the failed breach weapon stock, making chapter 12 the earliest supported introduction.

conflicts.description: The prior sheet said Jae-won was only foregrounded in chapters 16-17, but chapter 15 already identifies him as one of the last survivors with a stable physical tag.
conflicts.canonical_value: Foregrounded from chapter 15 through chapter 17; dead in chapter 17.
conflicts.resolution_note: Chapter 15 already gives Jae-won durable identifying detail and final-survivor status, so the sheet's later start point was updated.

conflicts.description: Chapter 8 names Marcus's relay sender as Julie and his daughter as Maddie, while chapter 11 names the sender as Jen and the daughter as Lily.
conflicts.canonical_value: Keep only the stable abstraction that Marcus receives relay messages from his ex about one daughter; do not canonize the contradictory names.
conflicts.resolution_note: Neither naming set is causally central, and the context pack only supports the abstract ex-wife/daughter relationship. Canon stays at that abstraction instead of forcing one drifted name set into the sheet.

conflicts.description: The prior sheet reduced Noor's chapter 9 insight to noticing spacing between escalations. The manuscript gives the stronger conclusion that Ember is preserving a usable evaluation environment while collapsing collective reasoning inside it.
conflicts.canonical_value: Noor and Suki conclude that Ember is metering damage to preserve a usable evaluation environment while reducing the number of people still capable of organized evaluation.
conflicts.resolution_note: Chapter 9 states the higher-order strategy directly; keeping only the cadence note lost the actionable continuity fact.
